title: Dry-wet regime split
summary: Pit-stop research notes wet/intermediate decisions are reactive and noisier, while dry-compound stops are more predictable from tyre degradation, stint length, and track position.
rationale: Pit-stop research notes wet/intermediate decisions are reactive and noisier, while dry-compound stops are more predictable from tyre degradation, stint length, and track position. Treating all compounds with one model may blur different mechanisms.
implementation_hint: Add regime flags: dry tyre, wet/intermediate, unknown/rare compound, soft-family compound. Train separate models or add high-order interactions for dry vs wet regimes; optionally downweight or isolate wet/intermediate rows if they are rare and unpredictable.
expected_effect: Sharper dry-strategy ranking and less confusion from reactive weather-driven stops.
risk: If test contains many wet/intermediate laps, downweighting may hurt; compare full-model blend versus dry-focused model rather than hard filtering only.
